generatedSentence: In a remote village nestled between active volcanoes, the wake of aggressive marketization left its inhabitants weaker, a guilty truth next door to prosperity, as labor for mackerel catches became jammed by an increase in aluminum tariffs; a dreamer in hackney twill used a notepad to find an empowering solution, sketching vivid, vertical galaxy maps on the verge of an SOS, fearing cosmic needles would exterminate their ostriches with mallets. The dreamer, no longer jovial, discovered an incredible, limitless power source, an ancient inheritance protected by home-made candles in an abstract chamber; utterly aware of potential abuse, a mandatory recheck of the intranet banking protocols for oil checking was done every century, lest any mortal dare absorb its energy with a lance, a thought that made the guard harrumph and reach for a sonic extinguisher to ensure fair play. Suddenly, a notorious warlock, known for his domination, launched a kickoff from an amphitheatre, his barking orders echoing as a verdict of war; a nymph, resistant to his will, tried to unjoin his spell while seventy mini magnets on a daffodil-shaped bottle flew higher towards a colossal windmill, attempting a pickoff, as the clock ticked calmly, the imagery of nylon nets and revelry faked to foresee his next move. After nightfall, liberation emerged, a vivid transition from the faked wars and ripoffs of the warlock's reign, the lawn now glowing with luminous honeycomb structures; they vividly juxtapose the old dial-up pyramids and wicker chairs as the people gather, their mini jukebox playing, a pickle jar now filled with gasoline as a symbolic extinguisher, sniffed out the last traces of evil, ensuring no one would underestimate the omniscient power of those who refuse to wait for justice.
panelSentences: In a remote village nestled between active volcanoes, the wake of aggressive marketization left its inhabitants weaker, a guilty truth next door to prosperity, as labor for mackerel catches became jammed by an increase in aluminum tariffs; a dreamer in hackney twill used a notepad to find an empowering solution, sketching vivid, vertical galaxy maps on the verge of an SOS, fearing cosmic needles would exterminate their ostriches with mallets.

The dreamer, no longer jovial, discovered an incredible, limitless power source, an ancient inheritance protected by home-made candles in an abstract chamber; utterly aware of potential abuse, a mandatory recheck of the intranet banking protocols for oil checking was done every century, lest any mortal dare absorb its energy with a lance, a thought that made the guard harrumph and reach for a sonic extinguisher to ensure fair play.

Suddenly, a notorious warlock, known for his domination, launched a kickoff from an amphitheatre, his barking orders echoing as a verdict of war; a nymph, resistant to his will, tried to unjoin his spell while seventy mini magnets on a daffodil-shaped bottle flew higher towards a colossal windmill, attempting a pickoff, as the clock ticked calmly, the imagery of nylon nets and revelry faked to foresee his next move.

After nightfall, liberation emerged, a vivid transition from the faked wars and ripoffs of the warlock's reign, the lawn now glowing with luminous honeycomb structures; they vividly juxtapose the old dial-up pyramids and wicker chairs as the people gather, their mini jukebox playing, a pickle jar now filled with gasoline as a symbolic extinguisher, sniffed out the last traces of evil, ensuring no one would underestimate the omniscient power of those who refuse to wait for justice.
rawPanelSentences: In a remote village nestled between active volcanoes, the wake of aggressive marketization left its inhabitants weaker, a guilty truth next door to prosperity, as labor for mackerel catches became jammed by an increase in aluminum tariffs; a dreamer in hackney twill used a notepad to find an empowering solution, sketching vivid, vertical galaxy maps on the verge of an SOS, fearing cosmic needles would exterminate their ostriches with mallets.

The dreamer, no longer jovial, discovered an incredible, limitless power source, an ancient inheritance protected by home-made candles in an abstract chamber; utterly aware of potential abuse, a mandatory recheck of the intranet banking protocols for oil checking was done every century, lest any mortal dare absorb its energy with a lance, a thought that made the guard harrumph and reach for a sonic extinguisher to ensure fair play.

Suddenly, a notorious warlock, known for his domination, launched a kickoff from an amphitheatre, his barking orders echoing as a verdict of war; a nymph, resistant to his will, tried to unjoin his spell while seventy mini magnets on a daffodil-shaped bottle flew higher towards a colossal windmill, attempting a pickoff, as the clock ticked calmly, the imagery of nylon nets and revelry faked to foresee his next move.

After nightfall, liberation emerged, a vivid transition from the faked wars and ripoffs of the warlock's reign, the lawn now glowing with luminous honeycomb structures; they vividly juxtapose the old dial-up pyramids and wicker chairs as the people gather, their mini jukebox playing, a pickle jar now filled with gasoline as a symbolic extinguisher, sniffed out the last traces of evil, ensuring no one would underestimate the omniscient power of those who refuse to wait for justice.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist is a young, determined figure with a luminous holographic notepad strapped to their forearm and wearing a flowing crimson cloak with intricate circuit patterns. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, a lone figure in a village of vertical aluminum structures nestled between glowing volcanoes, intently sketching a galaxy map on their arm-mounted notepad, a close-up, slightly low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, illuminated by flickering home-made candles, standing before an abstract, crystalline power source, a wide shot from a medium angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A dynamic battle scene inside a grand, futuristic amphitheatre; a defiant nymph attempts to disrupt a warlock's spell while glowing magnets fly towards a colossal windmill, a high-angle shot looking down on the chaos. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A tranquil yet vibrant cityscape at night, featuring luminous honeycomb buildings alongside ancient glowing pyramids, with the protagonist calmly observing the scene of peaceful gathering and liberation, a medium shot from a slightly elevated perspective.

definitions.definition: A rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town.
definitions.example: There are 2 churches and 3 shops in our village.

definitions.definition: A rural habitation that has a church, but no market.
A planned community such as a retirement community or shopping district.
A gated community.

A vent or fissure on the surface of a planet (usually in a mountainous form) with a magma chamber attached to the mantle of a planet or moon, periodically erupting forth lava and volcanic gases onto the surface.
A form of firework.
An ordinary horse.
A carriage for hire or a cab.
A horse used to ride or drive.
A breed of English horse.
A hired drudge; a hireling; a prostitute.
To make uninteresting or trite by frequent use.
To use as a hackney.
To carry in a hackney coach.
Offered for hire; hence, much used; trite; mean.
definitions.example: hackney authors

definitions.definition: An edible fish of the family Scombridae, often speckled.
A type of hammer with a larger-than-usual head made of wood, rubber or similar non-iron material, used by woodworkers for driving a tool, such as a chisel. A kind of maul.
definitions.example: Carpenters use mallets for assembling.

definitions.definition: A weapon resembling the tool, but typically much larger.
A small hammer-like tool used for playing certain musical instruments.
A light beetle with a long handle used in playing croquet.
The stick used to strike the ball in the sport of polo.
The act of waking, or state of being awake.
The state of forbearing sleep, especially for solemn or festive purposes; a vigil.
(often followed by up) To stop sleeping.
definitions.example: I woke up at four o'clock this morning.

definitions.definition: (often followed by up) To make somebody stop sleeping; to rouse from sleep.
definitions.example: The neighbour's car alarm woke me from a strange dream.

definitions.definition: To put in motion or action; to arouse; to excite.
To be excited or roused up; to be stirred up from a dormant, torpid, or inactive state; to be active.
To lay out a body prior to burial in order to allow family and friends to pay their last respects.
To watch, or sit up with, at night, as a dead body.
To be or remain awake; not to sleep.
To be alert; to keep watch
definitions.example: Command unto the guards that they diligently wake.

definitions.definition: To sit up late for festive purposes; to hold a night revel.
Lacking in force (usually strength) or ability.
definitions.example: The child was too weak to move the boulder.

definitions.definition: Unable to sustain a great weight, pressure, or strain.
definitions.example: a weak timber; a weak rope

definitions.definition: Unable to withstand temptation, urgency, persuasion, etc.; easily impressed, moved, or overcome; accessible; vulnerable.
definitions.example: weak resolutions; weak virtue

definitions.definition: Dilute, lacking in taste or potency.
definitions.example: We were served stale bread and weak tea.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Displaying a particular kind of inflection, including:
That does not ionize completely into anions and cations in a solution.
definitions.example: a weak acid;  a weak base

definitions.definition: One of the four fundamental forces associated with nuclear decay.
Bad or uncool.
definitions.example: This place is weak.

definitions.definition: Having a narrow range of logical consequences; narrowly applicable. (Often contrasted with a strong statement which implies it.)
Resulting from, or indicating, lack of judgment, discernment, or firmness; unwise; hence, foolish.
Not having power to convince; not supported by force of reason or truth; unsustained.
definitions.example: The prosecution advanced a weak case.

definitions.definition: Lacking in vigour or expression.
definitions.example: a weak sentence; a weak style

definitions.definition: Not prevalent or effective, or not felt to be prevalent; not potent; feeble.
Tending towards lower prices.
definitions.example: a weak market; wheat is weak at present

definitions.definition: Lacking contrast.
definitions.example: a weak negative

definitions.definition: A large flightless bird (Struthio camelus) native to Africa.
One who buries one's head in the sand instead of acknowledging problems
The Milky Way; the apparent band of concentrated stars which appears in the night sky over earth.
Any of the collections of many millions or billions of stars, galactic dust, black holes, etc. existing as independent and coherent systems, of which there are billions in the known universe.
Any print or pattern reminiscent of a galaxy, generally consisting of blending, semiopaque patches of vibrant color on a dark background.
To kill all of (a population of pests or undesirables), usually intentionally.
definitions.example: We'll use poison to exterminate the rats.

definitions.definition: To bring a definite end to; finish completely.
definitions.example: The public school failed to exterminate truancy.

definitions.definition: A fine, sharp implement usually for piercing such as sewing, or knitting, acupuncture, tattooing, body piercing, medical injections, etc.
definitions.example: The seamstress threaded the needle to sew on a button.

definitions.definition: Any slender, pointed object resembling a needle, such as a pointed crystal, a sharp pinnacle of rock, an obelisk, etc.
A fine measurement indicator on a dial or graph, e.g. a compass needle.
definitions.example: The needle on the fuel gauge pointed to empty.

definitions.definition: A sensor for playing phonograph records, a phonograph stylus.
definitions.example: Ziggy bought some diamond needles for his hi-fi phonograph.

definitions.definition: A needle-like leaf found on some conifers.
A strong beam resting on props, used as a temporary support during building repairs.
(usually preceded by the) The death penalty carried out by lethal injection.
A text string that is searched for within another string. (see: needle in a haystack)
To pierce with a needle, especially for sewing or acupuncture.
To tease in order to provoke; to poke fun at.
definitions.example: Billy needled his sister incessantly about her pimples.

definitions.definition: To form, or be formed, in the shape of a needle.
definitions.example: to needle crystals

definitions.definition: An amount by which a quantity is increased.
For a quantity, the act or process of becoming larger
Offspring, progeny
The creation of one or more new stitches; see Increase (knitting).
(of a quantity, etc.) To become larger or greater.
definitions.example: His rage only increased when I told him of the lost money.

definitions.definition: To make (a quantity, etc.) larger.
To multiply by the production of young; to be fertile, fruitful, or prolific.
To become more nearly full; to show more of the surface; to wax.
definitions.example: The Moon increases.

definitions.definition: A syllable used in solfège to represent the fifth note of a major scale.
To get something stuck in a confined space.
definitions.example: Her poor little baby toe got jammed in the door.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.
definitions.example: Jones was jammed by the pitch.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.
definitions.example: When he tripped on the step he jammed his toe.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
Stuck.
definitions.example: How to treat a jammed finger or toe.

definitions.definition: Overcrowded; congested
definitions.example: A jammed Oktoberfest beer tent.

definitions.definition: A felt-tipped permanent marker.
(of perception) Clear, detailed or powerful.
(of an image) Bright, intense or colourful.
Full of life, strikingly alive.
A plea by a defendant who does not contest a charge.
A verdict of a judge or jury on a defendant judged to have committed a crime.
One who is declared guilty of a crime.
Responsible for a dishonest act.
definitions.example: He was guilty of cheating at cards.

definitions.definition: Judged to have committed a crime.
definitions.example: The guilty man was led away.

definitions.definition: Having a sense of guilt.
definitions.example: Do you have a guilty conscience?

definitions.definition: Blameworthy.
definitions.example: I have a guilty secret.

definitions.definition: (usually postpositive) That is in an adjacent place; that is located next door.
definitions.example: Try the place next door.

definitions.definition: In an adjacent building, room or place.
Next door to.
Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.
That which requires hard work for its accomplishment; that which demands effort.
Workers in general; the working class, the workforce; sometimes specifically the labour movement, organised labour.
A political party or force aiming or claiming to represent the interests of labour.
The act of a mother giving birth.
The time period during which a mother gives birth.
The pitching or tossing of a vessel which results in the straining of timbers and rigging.
An old measure of land area in Mexico and Texas, approximately 177 acres.
To toil, to work.
To belabour, to emphasise or expand upon (a point in a debate, etc).
definitions.example: I think we've all got the idea. There's no need to labour the point.

definitions.definition: To be oppressed with difficulties or disease; to do one's work under conditions which make it especially hard or wearisome; to move slowly, as against opposition, or under a burden.
To suffer the pangs of childbirth.
To pitch or roll heavily, as a ship in a turbulent sea.
A pattern, characterised by diagonal ridges, created by the regular interlacing of threads of the warp and weft during weaving.
A cloth or portion of cloth woven in such a pattern.
To weave (cloth, etc.) so as to produce the appearance of diagonal lines or ribs on the surface.
A vertex or zenith.
A vertical geometrical figure; a perpendicular.
An individual slat in a set of vertical blinds.
A vertical component of a structure.
A vertical market.
definitions.example: We offer specialised accounting software targeting various verticals.

definitions.definition: Standing, pointing, or moving straight up or down; along the direction of a plumb line; perpendicular to something horizontal.
In a two-dimensional Cartesian co-ordinate system, describing the axis y oriented normal (perpendicular, at right angles) to the horizontal axis x.
In a three-dimensional co-ordinate system, describing the axis z oriented normal (perpendicular, orthogonal) to the basic plane xy.
Of or pertaining to vertical markets.
(wine tasting) Involving different vintages of the same wine type from the same winery.
Of an interval: having the two notes sound simultaneously.
A metallic chemical element (symbol Al) with an atomic number of 13.
A single atom of this element.
Aircraft or other machinery made partially or wholly of aluminum.
Anything that is found (usually valuable), as objects on an archeological site or a person with talent.
The act of finding.
To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.
To encounter or discover something being searched for; to locate.
definitions.example: I found my car keys. They were under the couch.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To discover by study or experiment direct to an object or end.
definitions.example: Water is found to be a compound substance.

definitions.definition: To gain, as the object of desire or effort.
definitions.example: to find leisure; to find means

definitions.definition: To attain to; to arrive at; to acquire.
definitions.example: Looks like he found a new vehicle for himself!

definitions.definition: To point out.
definitions.example: He kept finding faults with my work.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To decide that, to discover that, to form the opinion that.
definitions.example: I find your argument unsatisfactory.

definitions.definition: To arrive at, as a conclusion; to determine as true; to establish.
definitions.example: to find a verdict; to find a true bill (of indictment) against an accused person

definitions.definition: To supply; to furnish.
definitions.example: to find food for workmen

definitions.definition: To provide for
definitions.example: He finds his nephew in money.

definitions.definition: To determine or judge.
definitions.example: The jury finds for the defendant.

definitions.definition: To discover game.
To give permission, power, or the legal right to do something.
To give someone more confidence and/or strength to do something, often by enabling them to increase their control over their own life or situation.
definitions.example: John found that starting up his own business empowered him greatly in social situations.

definitions.definition: That empowers.
A pad of paper, often bound, in which one jots down notes; a notebook.
The exposure of service industries to market forces.
A restructuring process that enables state enterprises to operate as market-oriented firms by changing the legal environment in which they operate.
Cheerful and good-humoured; jolly, merry.
Pertaining to the astrological influence of the planet Jupiter; having the characteristics of a person under such influence (see sense 1).
(auxiliary) A syntactic marker.
To perform; to execute.
definitions.example: All you ever do is surf the Internet. What will you do this afternoon?

definitions.definition: To cause, make (someone) (do something).
To suffice.
definitions.example: it’s not the best broom, but it will have to do;  this will do me, thanks.

definitions.definition: To be reasonable or acceptable.
definitions.example: It simply will not do to have dozens of children running around such a quiet event.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To have (as an effect).
definitions.example: The fresh air did him some good.

definitions.definition: To fare, perform (well or poorly).
definitions.example: Our relationship isn't doing very well;  how do you do?

definitions.definition: (chiefly in questions) To have as one's job.
definitions.example: What does Bob do? — He's a plumber.

definitions.definition: To perform the tasks or actions associated with (something).
definitions.example: "Don't forget to do your report" means something quite different depending on whether you're a student or a programmer.

definitions.definition: To cook.
definitions.example: I'll just do some eggs.

definitions.definition: To travel in, to tour, to make a circuit of.
definitions.example: Let’s do New York also.

definitions.definition: To treat in a certain way.
To work for or on, by way of caring for, looking after, preparing, cleaning, keeping in order, etc.
To act or behave in a certain manner; to conduct oneself.
To spend (time) in jail. (See also do time)
definitions.example: I did five years for armed robbery.

definitions.definition: To impersonate or depict.
definitions.example: They really laughed when he did Clinton, with a perfect accent and a leer.

definitions.definition: (with 'a' and the name of a person, place, event, etc.) To copy or emulate the actions or behaviour that is associated with the person or thing mentioned.
definitions.example: He did a Henry VIII and got married six times.

definitions.definition: To kill.
To deal with for good and all; to finish up; to undo; to ruin; to do for.
To punish for a misdemeanor.
definitions.example: He got done for speeding.

definitions.definition: To have sex with. (See also do it)
To cheat or swindle.
definitions.example: That guy just did me out of two hundred bucks!

definitions.definition: To convert into a certain form; especially, to translate.
definitions.example: the novel has just been done into English;  I'm going to do this play into a movie

definitions.definition: To finish.
To work as a domestic servant (with for).
(auxiliary) Used to form the present progressive of verbs.
To cash or to advance money for, as a bill or note.
(ditransitive) To make or provide.
definitions.example: Could you do me a burger with mayonnaise instead of ketchup?

definitions.definition: To injure (one's own body part).
To take drugs.
definitions.example: I do cocaine.

definitions.definition: (in the form be doing [somewhere]) To exist with a purpose or for a reason.
definitions.example: What's that car doing in our swimming pool?

definitions.definition: (of food) Ready, fully cooked.
definitions.example: As soon as the potatoes are done we can sit down and eat.

definitions.definition: Having completed or finished an activity.
definitions.example: He pushed his empty plate away, sighed and pronounced "I am done."

definitions.definition: Being exhausted or fully spent.
definitions.example: When the water is done we will only be able to go on for a few days.

definitions.definition: Without hope or prospect of completion or success.
definitions.example: He is done, after three falls there is no chance he will be able to finish.

definitions.definition: Fashionable, socially acceptable, tasteful.
definitions.example: I can't believe he just walked up and spoke to her like that, those kind of things just aren't done!

definitions.definition: Ability to do or undergo something.
(social) Ability to coerce, influence or control.
(physical) Effectiveness.
A large amount or number.
Any of the elementary forms or parts of machines: three primary (the lever, inclined plane, and pulley) and three secondary (the wheel-and-axle, wedge, and screw).
definitions.example: the mechanical powers

definitions.definition: A measure of the effectiveness that a force producing a physical effect has over time. If linear, the quotient of: (force multiplied by the displacement of or in an object) ÷ time. If rotational, the quotient of: (force multiplied by the angle of displacement) ÷ time.

A product of equal factors (and generalizations of this notion): x^n, read as "x to the power of n" or the like, is called a power and denotes the product x \times x \times \cdots \times x, where x appears n times in the product; x is called the base and n the exponent.
Cardinality.
The probability that a statistical test will reject the null hypothesis when the alternative hypothesis is true.
(in plural) In Christian angelology, an intermediate level of angels, ranked above archangels, but exact position varies by classification scheme.
To provide power for (a mechanical or electronic device).
definitions.example: This CD player is powered by batteries.

definitions.definition: To hit or kick something forcefully.
To enable or provide the impetus for.
Impressive.

A period of 100 consecutive years; often specifically a numbered period with conventional start and end dates, e.g., the twentieth century, which stretches from (strictly) 1901 through 2000, or (informally) 1900 through 1999. The first century AD was from 1 to 100.

A unit in ancient Roman army, originally of 100 army soldiers as part of a cohort, later of more varied sizes (but typically containing 60 to 70 or 80) soldiers or other men (guards, police, firemen), commanded by a centurion.
A political division of ancient Rome, meeting in the Centuriate Assembly.
A hundred things of the same kind; a hundred.
A hundred runs scored either by a single player in one innings, or by two players in a partnership.
A score of one hundred points.
definitions.example: That was his tenth professional century.

definitions.definition: A race a hundred units (as meters, kilometres, miles) in length.
A banknote in the denomination of one hundred dollars.
A private computer network, typically one that uses the protocols of the Internet.
Too implausible to be credible; beyond belief; unbelievable.
Amazing; astonishing; awe-inspiring.
definitions.example: He was so wrapped up in watching the incredible special effects that he couldn't keep track of the story.

definitions.definition: Marvellous; profoundly affecting; wonderful.
definitions.example: I had such an incredible slice of pizza last night that I simply can't think about anything else.

definitions.definition: One who dreams.
Someone whose beliefs are far from realistic.
Any anglerfish of the family Oneirodidae.
Swallow-wing puffbird (Chelidoptera tenebrosa)
A light source consisting of a wick embedded in a solid, flammable substance such as wax, tallow, or paraffin.
The protruding, removable portion of a filter, particularly a water filter.
A unit of luminous intensity, now replaced by the SI unit candela.
A fast-growing, light-colored, upward-growing shoot on a pine tree in the spring. As growth slows in summer, the shoot darkens and is no longer conspicuous.
To observe the growth of an embryo inside (an egg), using a bright light source.
To dry greenware prior to beginning of the firing cycle, setting the kiln at 200° Celsius until all water is removed from the greenware.
To check an item (such as an envelope) by holding it between a light source and the eye.
An abridgement or summary of a longer publication.
Something that concentrates in itself the qualities of a larger item, or multiple items.
An abstraction; an abstract term; that which is abstract.
The theoretical way of looking at things; something that exists only in idealized form.
An abstract work of art.
A summary title of the key points detailing a tract of land, for ownership; abstract of title.
To separate; to disengage.
To remove; to take away; withdraw.
To steal; to take away; to remove without permission.
To summarize; to abridge; to epitomize.

To conceptualize an ideal subgroup by means of the generalization of an attribute, as follows: by apprehending an attribute inherent to one individual, then separating that attribute and contemplating it by itself, then conceiving of that attribute as a general quality, then despecifying that conceived quality with respect to several or many individuals, and by then ideating a group composed of those individuals perceived to possess said quality.
To extract by means of distillation.
To consider abstractly; to contemplate separately or by itself; to consider theoretically; to look at as a general quality.
To withdraw oneself; to retire.
To draw off (interest or attention).
definitions.example: He was wholly abstracted by other objects.

definitions.definition: To perform the process of abstraction.
To create abstractions.
To produce an abstraction, usually by refactoring existing code. Generally used with "out".
definitions.example: He abstracted out the square root function.

definitions.definition: Derived; extracted.
Drawn away; removed from; apart from; separate.
Not concrete: conceptual, ideal.
Difficult to understand; abstruse; hard to conceptualize.
Separately expressing a property or attribute of an object that is considered to be inherent to that object: attributive, ascriptive.

Pertaining comprehensively to, or representing, a class or group of objects, as opposed to any specific object; considered apart from any application to a particular object: general, generic, nonspecific; representational.
Absent-minded.
Pertaining to the formal aspect of art, such as the lines, colors, shapes, and the relationships among them.
Insufficiently factual.
Apart from practice or reality; vague; theoretical; impersonal; not applied.
(grammar) As a noun, denoting an intangible as opposed to an object, place, or person.
Of a class in object-oriented programming, being a partial basis for subclasses rather than a complete template for objects.
Improper treatment or usage; application to a wrong or bad purpose; an unjust, corrupt or wrongful practice or custom.
definitions.example: All abuse, whether physical, verbal, psychological or sexual, is bad.

definitions.definition: Misuse; improper use; perversion.
A delusion; an imposture; misrepresentation; deception.
Coarse, insulting speech; abusive language; language that unjustly or angrily vilifies.
Catachresis.
Physical maltreatment; injury; cruel treatment.
Violation; defilement; rape; forcing of undesired sexual activity by one person on another, often on a repeated basis.
The act of checking again; reverification.
definitions.example: The article needed a complete recheck.

definitions.definition: To check again.
A sign or line that require the path of the disc to be above, below or to one side of it.
A person, organisation or state who receives a mandate; a mandatary.
Obligatory; required or commanded by authority.
definitions.example: Attendance at a school is usually mandatory for children.

definitions.definition: Of, being or relating to a mandate.
definitions.example: Mandatory Palestine

definitions.definition: A human; someone susceptible to death.
definitions.example: Her wisdom was beyond that of a mere mortal.

definitions.definition: Susceptible to death by aging, sickness, injury, or wound; not immortal.
Causing death; deadly, fatal, killing, lethal (now only of wounds, injuries etc.).
Punishable by death.
Fatally vulnerable.
Of or relating to the time of death.
Affecting as if with power to kill; deathly.
definitions.example: mortal enemy

definitions.definition: Human; belonging or pertaining to people who are mortal.
definitions.example: mortal wit or knowledge; mortal power

definitions.definition: Very painful or tedious; wearisome.
definitions.example: a sermon lasting two mortal hours

definitions.definition: Very drunk; wasted; smashed.
Of a sin: involving the penalty of spiritual death, rather than merely venial.
Mortally; enough to cause death.
definitions.example: It's mortal cold out there.

definitions.definition: An expression of disdain, disbelief, protest, or dismissal; a huff, grunt, or snort.
definitions.example: Give the governor a harumph.

definitions.definition: To dislike, protest, or dismiss.
An expression of disdain, disbelief, protest, refusal or dismissal.
definitions.example: Harumph! She is welcome to try, but I'm not going to bother helping.

definitions.definition: Made at home.
definitions.example: a homemade chicken casserole

definitions.definition: Made by oneself.
definitions.example: a homemade bomb

definitions.definition: In a simple style as if made at home.
Something which is fair (in various senses of the adjective).
definitions.example: When will we learn to distinguish between the fair and the foul?

definitions.definition: A woman, a member of the ‘fair sex’; also as a collective singular, women.
Fairness, beauty.
A fair woman; a sweetheart.
Good fortune; good luck.
To smoothen or even a surface (especially a connection or junction on a surface).
To bring into perfect alignment (especially about rivet holes when connecting structural members).
To construct or design a structure whose primary function is to produce a smooth outline or reduce air drag or water resistance.
To make fair or beautiful.
Beautiful, of a pleasing appearance, with a pure and fresh quality.
definitions.example: Monday's child is fair of face.

definitions.definition: Unblemished (figuratively or literally); clean and pure; innocent.
definitions.example: After scratching out and replacing various words in the manuscript, he scribed a fair copy to send to the publisher.

definitions.definition: Light in color, pale, particularly with regard to skin tone but also referring to blond hair.
definitions.example: She had fair hair and blue eyes.

definitions.definition: Just, equitable.
definitions.example: He must be given a fair trial.

definitions.definition: Adequate, reasonable, or decent.
definitions.example: The patient was in a fair condition after some treatment.

definitions.definition: (of a wind) Favorable to a ship's course.
Not overcast; cloudless; clear; pleasant; propitious; said of the sky, weather, or wind, etc.
definitions.example: a fair sky;  a fair day

definitions.definition: Free from obstacles or hindrances; unobstructed; unencumbered; open; direct; said of a road, passage, etc.
definitions.example: a fair mark;  in fair sight;  a fair view

definitions.definition: Without sudden change of direction or curvature; smooth; flowing; said of the figure of a vessel, and of surfaces, water lines, and other lines.
Between the baselines.
(of a catch) Taken direct from an opponent's foot, without the ball touching the ground or another player.
(of a ball delivered by the bowler) Not a no-ball.
Of a coin or die, having equal chance of landing on any side, unbiased.
Clearly, openly, frankly, civilly, honestly, favorably, auspiciously, agreeably
A weapon of war, consisting of a long shaft or handle and a steel blade or head; a spear carried by horsemen.
A wooden spear, sometimes hollow, used in jousting or tilting, designed to shatter on impact with the opposing knight’s armour.
A spear or harpoon used by whalers and fishermen.
A soldier armed with a lance; a lancer.
An instrument which conveys the charge of a piece of ordnance and forces it home.
(founding) A small iron rod which suspends the core of the mold in casting a shell.
One of the small paper cases filled with combustible composition, which mark the outlines of a figure.
A lancet.
To pierce with a lance, or with any similar weapon.
definitions.example: Seized the due victim, and with fury lanced Her back. Dryden.

definitions.definition: To open with a lancet; to pierce
definitions.example: to lance a vein or an abscess

definitions.definition: To throw in the manner of a lance; to lanch.
Without limits in extent, size, or quantity; boundless.
Completely, entirely, to the fullest extent
definitions.example: I have failed you utterly.

definitions.definition: Liquid fat.
Petroleum-based liquid used as fuel or lubricant.
An oil painting.
Oil paint.
definitions.example: I prefer to paint in oil

definitions.definition: To deal with a bank or financial institution, or for an institution to provide financial services to a client.
definitions.example: He banked with Barclays.

definitions.definition: To put into a bank.
definitions.example: I'm going to bank the money.

definitions.definition: To conceal in the rectum for use in prison.
definitions.example: Johnny banked some coke for me.

definitions.definition: To roll or incline laterally in order to turn.
To cause (an aircraft) to bank.
To form into a bank or heap, to bank up.
definitions.example: to bank sand

definitions.definition: To cover the embers of a fire with ashes in order to retain heat.
To raise a mound or dike about; to enclose, defend, or fortify with a bank; to embank.
To pass by the banks of.
To provide additional power for a train ascending a bank (incline) by attaching another locomotive.
(order and arrangement) To arrange or order in a row.
The business of managing a bank.
The occupation of managing or working in a bank.
A horizontal turn.
A mechanical component to prevent vibration in a timepiece, etc.
The practice of assisting a train up a steep incline (called a bank) with another locomotive at the rear.
To inspect; to examine.
definitions.example: Check the oil in your car once a month.

definitions.definition: To verify the accuracy of a text or translation, usually making some corrections (proofread) or many (copyedit).

(often used with "off") To mark items on a list (with a checkmark or by crossing them out) that have been chosen for keeping or removal or that have been dealt with (for example, completed or verified as correct or satisfactory).
definitions.example: Check off the items that you've checked (inspected).

definitions.definition: To control, limit, or halt.
definitions.example: Check your enthusiasm during a negotiation.

definitions.definition: To verify or compare with a source of information.
definitions.example: Check your data against known values.

definitions.definition: To leave in safekeeping.
definitions.example: Check your hat and coat at the door.

definitions.definition: To leave with a shipping agent for shipping.
definitions.example: Check your bags at the ticket counter before the flight.

definitions.definition: To pass or bounce the ball to an opponent from behind the three-point line and have the opponent pass or bounce it back to start play.
definitions.example: He checked the ball and then proceeded to perform a perfect layup.

definitions.definition: To disrupt another player with the stick or body to obtain possession of the ball or puck.
definitions.example: The hockey player checked the defenceman to obtain the puck.

definitions.definition: To remain in a hand without betting. Only legal if no one has yet bet.
definitions.example: Tom didn't think he could win, so he checked.

definitions.definition: To make a move which puts an adversary's king in check; to put in check.
To chide, rebuke, or reprove.
To slack or ease off, as a brace which is too stiffly extended.
To crack or gape open, as wood in drying; or to crack in small checks, as varnish, paint, etc.
To make checks or chinks in; to cause to crack.
definitions.example: The sun checks timber.

definitions.definition: To make a stop; to pause; with at.
To clash or interfere.
To act as a curb or restraint.
To turn, when in pursuit of proper game, and fly after other birds.
To mark with a check pattern.
The act of physically keeping an opposing player in check.
A checking account.
definitions.example: Withdraw $5000 from checking and put it into savings.

definitions.definition: To include so that it no longer has separate existence; to overwhelm; to cause to disappear as if by swallowing up; to incorporate; to assimilate; to take in and use up.
To engulf, as in water; to swallow up.
To suck up; to drink in; to imbibe, like a sponge or as the lacteals of the body; to chemically take in.
To take in energy and convert it, as
definitions.example: Heat, light, and electricity are absorbed in the substances into which they pass.

definitions.definition: To engross or engage wholly; to occupy fully
To occupy or consume time.
Assimilate mentally.
To assume or pay for as part of a commercial transaction.
To defray the costs.
To accept or purchase in quantity.
One who, or that which, extinguishes something.
The passing of title to an estate upon death.
That which a person is entitled to inherit, by law or testament.
The act or mechanism of inheriting; the state of having inherited
definitions.example: The Indo-European languages share various similarities as a result of their inheritance from a common ancestor.

definitions.definition: (genetic algorithms) The biological attributes passed hereditarily from ancestors to their offspring.
The mechanism whereby parts of a superclass are available to instances of its subclass.
A piece of material that attracts some metals by magnetism.
(preceded by a noun) A person or thing that attracts what is denoted by the preceding noun.
definitions.example: He always had a girl on his arm – he's a bit of a babe magnet.

definitions.definition: A bulbous plant of the genus Narcissus, with yellow flowers and a trumpet shaped corona, especially Narcissus pseudonarcissus, the national flower of Wales.
A brilliant yellow color, like that of a daffodil.
Of a brilliant yellow color, like that of a daffodil.
A container, typically made of glass or plastic and having a tapered neck, used primarily for holding liquids.
definitions.example: Beer is often sold in bottles.

definitions.definition: The contents of such a container.
definitions.example: I only drank a bottle of beer.

definitions.definition: A container with a rubber nipple used for giving liquids to infants, a baby bottle.
definitions.example: The baby wants a bottle.

definitions.definition: Nerve, courage.
definitions.example: He was going to ask her out, but he lost his bottle when he saw her.

definitions.definition: (of a person with a particular hair color) A container of hair dye, hence with one’s hair color produced by dyeing.
definitions.example: Did you know he’s a bottle brunette? His natural hair color is strawberry blonde.

definitions.definition: A bundle, especially of hay; something tied in a bundle.
Intoxicating liquor; alcohol.
definitions.example: to drown one’s troubles in the bottle

definitions.definition: The tendency of pages printed several on a sheet to rotate slightly when the sheet is folded two or more times.
To seal (a liquid) into a bottle for later consumption. Also fig.
definitions.example: This plant bottles vast quantities of spring water every day.

definitions.definition: To feed (an infant) baby formula.
definitions.example: Because of complications she can't breast feed her baby and so she bottles him.

definitions.definition: To refrain from doing (something) at the last moment because of a sudden loss of courage.
definitions.example: The rider bottled the big jump.

definitions.definition: To throw away a leading position.
definitions.example: Liverpool bottled the Premier League.

definitions.definition: To strike (someone) with a bottle.
definitions.example: He was bottled at a nightclub and had to have facial surgery.

definitions.definition: To pelt (a musical act on stage, etc.) with bottles as a sign of disapproval.
definitions.example: Meat Loaf was once bottled at Reading Festival.

definitions.definition: Very elevated; extending or being far above a base; tall; lofty.
definitions.example: The balloon rose high in the sky.   The wall was high.   a high mountain

definitions.definition: Relatively elevated; rising or raised above the average or normal level from which elevation is measured.
Having a specified elevation or height; tall.
definitions.example: three feet high   three Mount Everests high

definitions.definition: Elevated in status, esteem, prestige; exalted in rank, station, or character.
definitions.example: The oldest of the elves' royal family still conversed in High Elvish.

definitions.definition: Of great importance and consequence: grave (if negative) or solemn (if positive).
definitions.example: high crimes, the high festival of the sun

definitions.definition: Consummate; advanced (e.g. in development) to the utmost extent or culmination, or possessing a quality in its supreme degree, at its zenith.
definitions.example: high (i.e. intense) heat; high (i.e. full or quite) noon; high (i.e. rich or spicy) seasoning; high (i.e. complete) pleasure; high (i.e. deep or vivid) colour; high (i.e. extensive, thorough) scholarship; high tide; high [tourism] season; the High Middle Ages

definitions.definition: (in several set phrases) Remote in distance or time.
definitions.example: high latitude, high antiquity

definitions.definition: (in several set phrases) Very traditionalist and conservative, especially in favoring older ways of doing things; see e.g. high church, High Tory.
Elevated in mood; marked by great merriment, excitement, etc.

definitions.definition: (of a lifestyle) Luxurious; rich.
definitions.example: high living, the high life

definitions.definition: Lofty, often to the point of arrogant, haughty, boastful, proud.

definitions.definition: (with "on" or "about") Keen, enthused.
(of a body of water) With tall waves.
Large, great (in amount or quantity, value, force, energy, etc).
definitions.example: My bank charges me a high interest rate.   I was running a high temperature and had high cholesterol.   high voltage   high prices   high winds   a high number

definitions.definition: (acoustics) Acute or shrill in pitch, due to being of greater frequency, i.e. produced by more rapid vibrations (wave oscillations).
definitions.example: The note was too high for her to sing.

definitions.definition: Made with some part of the tongue positioned high in the mouth, relatively close to the palate.
Greater in value than other cards, denominations, suits, etc.
(of meat, especially venison) Strong-scented; slightly tainted/spoiled; beginning to decompose.
definitions.example: Epicures do not cook game before it is high.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated; under the influence of a mood-altering drug, formerly usually alcohol, but now (from the mid-20th century) usually not alcohol but rather marijuana, cocaine, heroin, etc.
(of a sailing ship) Near, in its direction of travel, to the (direction of the) wind.
In or to an elevated position.

definitions.definition: In or at a great value.
definitions.example: Costs have grown higher this year again.

definitions.definition: At a pitch of great frequency.
definitions.example: I certainly can't sing that high.

definitions.definition: A national school-leaving examination and university entrance qualification.
To make higher; to raise or increase in amount or quantity.
To ascend.

An open, outdoor theatre (which may be a theatre in the round, or have a stage with seating on only one side), especially one from the classical period of ancient Greece or Rome, or a modern venue of similar design.
definitions.example: Ancient Roman amphitheatres were mostly oval or circular in plan, with seating tiers that surrounded the central performance area, like a modern open-air stadium.

definitions.definition: A natural formation of a similar shape, where a steep mountain or slope a particular rock formation forms a partial or compete bowl, especially one used as a performance space (and possibly modified by carving out seats, etc) because the slopes naturally amplify or echo sound.

Originally, the DuPont company trade name for polyamide, a copolymer whose molecules consist of alternating diamine and dicarboxylic acid monomers bonded together; now generically used for this type of polymer.
(in the plural) A stocking originally fabricated from nylon; also used generically for any long, sheer stocking worn on a woman's legs.

definitions.definition: (Greek, Roman) Any female nature spirit associated with water, forests, grotto, wind, etc.
A young girl, especially one who is attractive, beautiful or graceful.
The larva of certain insects.
Any of various butterflies of the family Nymphalidae.
Joyful or riotous merry-making.
To separate or detach (things that were joined).
To cease to be a member of; to leave.
To make (something) better; to increase the value or productivity (of something).

definitions.definition: To become better.

definitions.definition: To disprove or make void; to refute.
To disapprove of; to find fault with; to reprove; to censure.

definitions.definition: To use or employ to good purpose; to turn to profitable account.

definitions.definition: The work of one who makes images or visible representation of objects.
Imitation work.
Images in general, or en masse.
Unreal show; imitation; appearance.
The work of the imagination or fancy; false ideas; imaginary phantasms.
Rhetorical decoration in writing or speaking; vivid descriptions presenting or suggesting images of sensible objects; figures in discourse.
To pronounce.

definitions.definition: To recite.

definitions.definition: To tell, either verbally or in writing.

definitions.definition: To indicate in a written form.

definitions.definition: To have a common expression; used in singular passive voice or plural active voice to indicate a rumor or well-known fact.

definitions.definition: Suppose, assume; used to mark an example, supposition or hypothesis.
definitions.example: A holiday somewhere warm – Florida, say – would be nice.

definitions.definition: To speak; to express an opinion; to make answer; to reply.
(of a possession, especially money) To bet as a wager on an outcome; by extension, used to express belief in an outcome by the speaker.
To try; to assay.
A proverb or maxim.
That which is said; a statement.
The cardinal number occurring after sixty-nine and before seventy-one, represented in Roman numerals as LXX and in Arabic numerals as 70.
A seat (tack) for a rider placed on the back of a horse or other animal.
An item of harness (harness saddle) placed on the back of a horse or other animal.
A seat on a bicycle, motorcycle, etc.
A cut of meat that includes both loins and part of the backbone.
A low point, in the shape of a saddle, between two hills.
A formation of gold-bearing quartz occurring along the crest of an anticlinal fold, especially in Australia.
The raised floorboard in a doorway.
A small tapered or sloped area structure that helps channel surface water to drains.
A block of wood, usually fastened to one spar and shaped to receive the end of another.
A part, such as a flange, which is hollowed out to fit upon a convex surface and serve as a means of attachment or support.
The clitellum of an earthworm.
Any of the saddle-like markings on a boa constrictor.
A saddle shoe.
A lid.
Area or situation which screens a person or thing from view.

definitions.definition: The front and back of a book, magazine, CD package, etc.
The top sheet of a bed.
A cloth, usually fitted, placed over an item such as a car or sofa to protect it from dust, rain, etc. when not in use.
A cover charge.

definitions.definition: A new performance or rerecording of a previously recorded song; a cover version; a cover song.
A fielding position on the off side, between point and mid off, about 30° forward of square; a fielder in this position.
A set (more often known as a family) of sets, whose union contains the given set.
definitions.example: The open intervals are a cover for the real numbers.

definitions.definition: An envelope complete with stamps and postmarks etc.
A solid object, including terrain, that provides protection from enemy fire.

In commercial law, a buyer’s purchase on the open market of goods similar or identical to the goods contracted for after a seller has breached a contract of sale by failure to deliver the goods contracted for.
An insurance contract; coverage by an insurance contract.
A persona maintained by a spy or undercover operative; cover story.
A swindler's confederate.
The portion of a slate, tile, or shingle that is hidden by the overlap of the course above.
In a steam engine, the lap of a slide valve.
The distance between reinforcing steel and the exterior of concrete.
The act of dominating; the exercise of power when ruling
A ruling party; a party in power.
A high order of angels in the celestial hierarchy.
A fetish characterized by control/power over and discipline of one's sexual partner.

A machine which translates linear motion of wind to rotational motion by means of adjustable vanes called sails.
The structure containing such machinery.
A child's toy consisting of vanes mounted on a stick that rotate when blown by a person or by the wind.
A dunk where the dunker swings his arm in a circular motion before throwing the ball through the hoop.
A guitar move where the strumming hand mimics a turning windmill.
A breakdancing move in which the dancer rolls his/her torso continuously in a circular path on the floor, across the upper chest, shoulders and back, while twirling the legs in a V shape in the air.
Any of various large papilionid butterflies of the genus Byasa, the wings of which resemble the vanes of a windmill.
The false shower.
An imaginary enemy, but presented as real.
To rotate with a sweeping motion.
definitions.example: She ran down the hill, windmilling her arms with glee.

definitions.definition: Of a rotating part of a machine, to (become disengaged and) rotate freely.
definitions.example: The axle broke and the wheel windmilled in place briefly before careening through the wall.

definitions.definition: To make a short, loud, explosive noise with the vocal organs (said of animals, especially dogs).

definitions.definition: To make a clamor; to make importunate outcries.
To speak sharply.
definitions.example: The sergeant barked an order.

definitions.definition: To strip the bark from; to peel.
To abrade or rub off any outer covering from.

definitions.definition: To girdle.
To cover or inclose with bark, or as with bark.

definitions.definition: Who or that barks or bark.

definitions.definition: Completely insane.
A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.
definitions.example: The jury returned a “not guilty” verdict.

definitions.definition: An opinion or judgement.
definitions.example: a “not out” verdict from the umpire

definitions.definition: In a calm manner.
An instrument used to measure or keep track of time; a non-portable timepiece.
The odometer of a motor vehicle.
definitions.example: This car has over 300,000 miles on the clock.

definitions.definition: An electrical signal that synchronizes timing among digital circuits of semiconductor chips or modules.
The seed head of a dandelion.
A time clock.

definitions.definition: A CPU clock cycle, or T-state.
To measure the duration of.
To measure the speed of.
definitions.example: He was clocked at 155 miles per hour.

definitions.definition: To hit (someone) heavily.
definitions.example: When the boxer let down his guard, his opponent clocked him.

definitions.definition: To take notice of; to realise; to recognize someone or something
definitions.example: A trans person may be able to easily clock other trans people.

definitions.example: I don't believe that car has done only 40,000 miles. It's been clocked.

definitions.definition: To beat a video game.

definitions.definition: The opening kick of each half of a game of football.
(by extension) the opening sequence of any event
To be able to see beforehand: to anticipate; predict.
To provide.
In a notorious or notable manner; as is commonly known.
A person who resists; especially a member of a resistance movement.
A thing which resists.
Which makes resistance or offers opposition.
Which is not affected or overcome by a disease, drug, chemical or atmospheric agent, extreme of temperature, etc.
definitions.example: The infection is resistant to antibiotics.

definitions.definition: Not greatly influenced by individual members of a sample.
The act of liberating or the state of being liberated.
The process of striving to achieve equal rights and status.
An open space between woods.
Ground (generally in front of or around a house) covered with grass kept closely mown.
An overgrown agar culture, such that no separation between single colonies exists.
An estimate that is too low.
To perceive (someone or something) as having a lower value, quantity, worth, etc., than what he/she/it actually has.
To make a short, audible inhalation, through the nose, as when smelling something.
definitions.example: I sniffed the meat to see whether it had gone off.

definitions.definition: To say something while sniffing, for example in case of illness or unhappiness, or in contempt.
definitions.example: "He's never coming back, is he?" she sniffed while looking at a picture of him.

definitions.definition: To perceive vaguely
definitions.example: I can sniff trouble coming from the basement.

definitions.definition: To be dismissive or contemptuous of something.
definitions.example: This opportunity is not to be sniffed at.

definitions.definition: To intercept and analyse packets of data being transmitted over a network.
To inhale drugs in powder form (usually cocaine) through the nose.
To place side by side, especially for contrast or comparison.
One who has total knowledge.
Having total knowledge.
definitions.example: The Abrahamic God is omniscient.

definitions.definition: The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.
A word or phrase connecting one part of a discourse to another.
A brief modulation; a passage connecting two themes.
A change of key.
A point mutation in which one base is replaced by another of the same class (purine or pyrimidine); compare transversion.
A change from defense to attack, or attack to defense.
The onset of the final stage of childbirth.
Professional special education assistance for children or adults in the process of leaving one educational environment or support program for another to relatively more independent living.
A change between forward and backward motion without stopping.
The process or act of changing from one gender role to another, or of bringing one's outward appearance in line with one's internal gender identity.
To make a transition.
To bring through a transition; to change.

definitions.definition: To change from one gender role to another, or bring one's outward appearance in line with one's internal gender identity.
In a vivid manner.
A structure of hexagonal cells made by bees primarily of wax, to hold their larvae and for storing the honey to feed the larvae and to feed themselves during winter.
(by extension) Any structure resembling a honeycomb.
definitions.example: The wood porch was a honeycomb of termite tunnels before we replaced it.

definitions.definition: Voids left in concrete resulting from failure of the mortar to effectively fill the spaces among coarse aggregate particles.
Manufactured material used manufacture light, stiff structural components using a sandwich design.
(solar cell) The texture of the surface of a solar cell, intended to increase its surface area and capture more sunlight.
A space-filling packing of polytopes in 3- or higher-dimensional space.
To riddle something with holes, especially in such a pattern.
definitions.example: Termites will honeycomb a porch made of untreated pine.

definitions.definition: To cheat; to swindle; to steal; to rob.
To modify fraudulently, so as to make an object appear better or other than it really is
To make a counterfeit, to counterfeit, to forge, to falsify.
To make a false display of, to affect, to feign, to simulate.

definitions.definition: To coil (a rope, line, or hawser), by winding alternately in opposite directions, in layers usually of zigzag or figure of eight form, to prevent twisting when running out.
A flammable liquid consisting of a mixture of refined petroleum hydrocarbons, mainly used as a motor fuel; petrol.
Any specific kind of gasoline.

definitions.definition: Made from or using gasoline.
A plait or fold in cloth, made by drawing a thread through it; a pucker.
The inclination forward of the axle journals to keep the wheels from working outward.
The soffit or under surface of the masonry required in gathering. See gather (transitive verb).
A blob of molten glass collected on the end of a blowpipe.
A gathering.
To collect; normally separate things.

definitions.definition: To bring parts of a whole closer.
definitions.example: She gathered the shawl about her as she stepped into the cold.

definitions.definition: To infer or conclude; to know from a different source.

definitions.definition: To collect molten glass on the end of a tool.
To gain; to win.
To come into view.
To come out of a situation, object or a liquid.

definitions.definition: To become known.

definitions.definition: The close of the day; the coming of night.
A bad deal; an unfair or exorbitant price or rate.
A theft or robbery.

Emitting light; glowing brightly.
Brightly illuminated.
Miniature, tiny, small.

(by extension) To play or repeat as if on a jukebox.
To format or set up for playing by a jukebox.
A network connection that requires a telephone number to be dialed.
(of a connection to a network, especially to the Internet) that requires a telephone number to be dialed
An ancient massive construction with a square or rectangular base and four triangular sides meeting in an apex, such as those built as tombs in Egypt or as bases for temples in Mesoamerica.
A construction in the shape of a pyramid, usually with a square or rectangular base.
A solid with triangular lateral faces and a polygonal (often square or rectangular) base.
A medullary pyramid, the medial-most bumps on the ventral side of the medulla oblongata
The game of pool in which the balls are placed in the form of a triangle at spot.
A pyramid scheme.
Alternative letter-case form of Pyramid. (a solitaire card game)
The triangular layout of cards in the game of Pyramid.
definitions.example: Build your pyramid with all cards face down, except the cards in the bottom row.

definitions.definition: An approximately triangular headline consisting of several centered lines of text of increasing length.
To build up or be arranged in the form of a pyramid.
To combine (a series of genes) into a single genotype.
To employ, or take part in, a pyramid scheme.
To engage in pyramid trading.
The game of pyramid pool.
A cucumber preserved in a solution, usually a brine or a vinegar syrup.
definitions.example: A pickle goes well with a hamburger.

definitions.definition: (often in the plural) Any vegetable preserved in vinegar and consumed as relish.
The brine used for preserving food.
definitions.example: This tub is filled with the pickle that we will put the small cucumbers into.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation; peril.
definitions.example: The climber found himself in a pickle when one of the rocks broke off.

definitions.definition: A mildly mischievous loved one.
A rundown.
definitions.example: Jones was caught in a pickle between second and third.

definitions.definition: A children’s game with three participants that emulates a baseball rundown
definitions.example: The boys played pickle in the front yard for an hour.

definitions.definition: A penis.
A pipe for smoking methamphetamine.
definitions.example: Load some shards in that pickle.

definitions.definition: A bath of dilute sulphuric or nitric acid, etc., to remove burnt sand, scale, rust, etc., from the surface of castings, or other articles of metal, or to brighten them or improve their colour.
In an optical landing system, the hand-held controller connected to the lens, or apparatus on which the lights are mounted.
To preserve food (or sometimes other things) in a salt, sugar or vinegar solution.
definitions.example: These cucumbers pickle very well.

definitions.definition: To remove high-temperature scale and oxidation from metal with heated (often sulphuric) industrial acid.
definitions.example: The crew will pickle the fittings in the morning.

definitions.definition: (in the Python programming language) To serialize.
To pour brine over a person after flogging them, as a method of punishment.
Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually involving the engagement of military forces.
definitions.example: The war was largely between Sunni and Shia militants.

definitions.definition: A particular conflict of this kind.
(by extension) Any conflict, or anything resembling a conflict.
definitions.example: You look like you've been through the wars.

definitions.definition: Instruments of war.
Armed forces.
A particular card game for two players, notable for having its outcome predetermined by how the cards are dealt.
To engage in conflict (may be followed by "with" to specify the foe).
To carry on, as a contest; to wage.
A male magic-user; a male witch.
A flexible branch or twig of a plant such as willow, used in weaving baskets and furniture
Wickerwork.
Made of wickerwork.
A male or female attendant who serves customers at their tables in a restaurant, café or similar.
definitions.example: Waiter! There's a fly in my soup.

definitions.definition: Someone who waits for somebody or something; a person who is waiting.
A person working as an attendant at the London Stock Exchange.
A vessel or tray on which something is carried, as dishes, etc.; a salver. (See etymology of dumbwaiter.)
A custom house officer; a tide waiter.
A watchman.
